title: Exception — Prevent Death or Substantial Bodily Harm
text: There is no privilege under this article if the lawyer reasonably believes that disclosure of any confidential communication relating to representation of a client is necessary to prevent a criminal act that the lawyer reasonably believes is likely to result in the death of, or substantial bodily harm to, an individual.

source.editorial_note: Amended by Stats. 2004, Ch. 183, Sec. 94. Effective January 1, 2005.
